assertions.title: Wallet-connect approval uses live agent only in unlocked state
assertions.reason: The focused wallet-connect Jest suite passed both tests, proving the locked deep-link path never submits a response while agent state is null and the unlocked approve path submits exactly once with the live agent plus a 4-digit connect PIN from CryptoUtils.randomPin.

summaryLines: Locked path keeps submitConnectResponse call count at 0 while agent is null.
Unlock + approve path calls submitConnectResponse once with the live agent and randomPin({ length: 4 }).
testNames: no submitConnectResponse call can happen while the session is locked (agent is null)
after biometric unlock, Approve calls submitConnectResponse with the live agent and randomPin

assertions.title: Auto-lock tears down agent on background; foreground requires biometric re-unlock
assertions.reason: The focused auto-lock Jest suite passed both tests, covering active→background lock plus teardown, empty in-memory agent state after teardown, no foreground auto-prompt, and a fresh NativeBiometricVault.getSecret call only after explicit unlock.

summaryLines: Background transition called both sessionStore.lock() and agentStore.teardown() once.
Foreground transition left the session locked without a new biometric getSecret call until explicit unlockAgent().
testNames: background drops agent + locks session; foreground does NOT auto-prompt biometrics
inactive → background transition (already non-active) does NOT double-teardown

assertions.title: `enbox://` URL scheme registration unchanged
assertions.reason: The URL registration Jest suite passed all three tests, and a direct ripgrep over Info.plist and AndroidManifest.xml confirmed the iOS CFBundleURLSchemes entry still contains enbox and Android MainActivity still exposes enbox://connect deep-link data.

summaryLines: AndroidManifest.xml still declares a VIEW/BROWSABLE deep link data tag with scheme enbox and host connect, and MainActivity remains exported.
testNames: iOS Info.plist still declares the `enbox` URL scheme
Android manifest still declares the intent-filter for enbox://connect
MainActivity is exported (required for deep-link resolution)

PASS; the focused auto-lock validation suite passed 2/2 tests.
PASS; the saved source scan captured the teardown, lock, and no-auto-prompt expectations used by VAL-CROSS-005.
PASS; the focused URL registration validation suite passed 3/3 tests.
PASS; the saved source scan captured the iOS and Android URL-registration assertions in the Jest file.

summary: Tested 3 assertions on the jest-local surface: 3 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Focused wallet-connect, auto-lock, and URL registration Jest suites all passed, and supporting source/file scans confirmed the locked approval gate, background teardown + fresh biometric re-unlock, and unchanged enbox URL registration.
